channel.title: Tri-County Driving Academy
channel.description: Job Training Programs & Career Training — SCHEV Certified, Lebanon VA

item.title: About Us
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
About Tri-County Driving Academy

Tri-County Driving Academy/Skill Center was established in 1996 by D. Carter McGlothlin. The organization started as a school for Class "A" CDL instruction. Over the years the Center has grown to include training for Heavy Equipment Operators and Diesel Mechanics/Heavy Truck Maintenance. The school provides personal, one-on-one training to each student.

We are located at 1714 US Hwy 19, Hansonville, Virginia — conveniently situated between Lebanon and Abingdon, VA, near I-81 Exit 14.

Our Instructors

Our instructors are certified in their fields and provide the "one-on-one" attention that ensures our students are job-ready before completing their training. All classes are fully equipped with up-to-date training materials to provide comprehensive, hands-on education in your area of interest.

Our Credentials

  • Certified by the State Council of Higher Education for Virginia (SCHEV)
  • Authorized for operation as a post-secondary educational institution by the Tennessee Higher Education Commission (THEC)
  • NCCER-affiliated training center for Heavy Equipment Operations

Career Placement

We assist with job placement in all areas. With the growing need for qualified tractor-trailer drivers, heavy equipment operators, and diesel mechanics, we actively work with employers to connect graduates with career opportunities. Many students receive job offers before they complete their training.

  • Individual students receive both classroom and hands-on job-site training
  • Students handle real equipment in a variety of real-world situations
  • Today's equipment complexity demands professional, well-trained operators
  • Companies depend on us to supply skilled, motivated employees

item.title: CDL Class "A" Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tri-County Driving Academy's Commercial Driver / Class "A" CDL Training program provides you with the knowledge and skills to become a licensed Class A CDL driver, qualifying you to drive long-haul, regionally, or for a local carrier. The typical pay scale for a Commercial Driver averages $2,400 – $3,600 monthly.

Tuition includes the cost of training, books, supplies, DOT medical physical, and drug screen.

What You Will Learn

  • Preparation for the Class A Commercial Written Exams
  • Class A Commercial Road Test preparation
  • Pre-trip Inspection
  • Backing Maneuvers (straight-line, 45-degree, parallel)
  • Coupling and Uncoupling
  • Defensive Driving and Safety
  • DOT Physical (included in tuition)

Training Schedule

The training program is four weeks. The first week is preparation for the Class A Commercial Driver's Learner Permit. During class hours you will also be scheduled to complete your DOT physical (included in tuition). The remaining weeks provide hands-on training in the trucks. DMV testing is completed during the last week.

Classes begin every three weeks — call (276) 794-7660 for the next available start date.

Job Placement

Tri-County Driving Academy actively works to place graduates with major carriers. Many graduates complete employment applications and interviews before graduation, allowing them to go to work immediately after training.

Admission Requirements

  • Valid Driver's License
  • DMV driving record (obtainable at your state DMV)
  • Must pass a DOT physical (included in tuition)
  • 18 years of age or older

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: $4,500 (VA) / $3,450 (TN) / $3,950 (other states) — includes training, books, supplies, DOT physical & drug screen
Classes begin every three weeks — call (276) 794-7660 for scheduling

item.title: Certified Heavy Equipment Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The construction industry is a thriving business in constant need of safe, competent heavy equipment operators. Companies are looking for professional, well-trained employees — and Tri-County Driving Academy can help you become one. The typical pay scale for Construction / Equipment Operators is $1,800 – $3,600 monthly. Many graduates enjoy full benefits including major medical and retirement programs.

Our Heavy Equipment Training program prepares students for entry-level positions as operators with various construction companies. Tuition includes tuition fee, books, supplies, and certifications.

Equipment & Skills Covered

  • Backhoe operation
  • Excavator operation
  • Dozer (bulldozer) operation
  • Blueprint reading
  • Industrial safety concepts
  • OSHA safety certification
  • Flagman certification
  • Forklift certification

NCCER Industry Credentials

Students successfully completing Heavy Equipment Operations Level One through Tri-County Driving Academy earn industry-recognized NCCER credentials verifiable through the NCCER Automated National Registry. This certification is recognized by employers across the construction industry nationwide.

Admission Requirements

  • Must be 18 years or older prior to the first day of class
  • Must be in sound physical health

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: $4,900 (VA) / $4,250 (TN & other states) — includes tuition, books, supplies & certifications

item.title: Diesel Mechanics / Heavy Truck Maintenance
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The need for diesel mechanics is increasing as the diesel engine becomes standard in most of the nation's trucks, locomotives, and buses. The median hourly wage for Diesel Engine Specialists is approximately $18.94/hour (BLS), with experienced technicians earning significantly more.

Our 11-month program provides hands-on training and lab/classroom instruction, building a foundation in the latest diesel technology service and repair. Tuition includes training, books, supplies, and first testing fees for ASE Certification.

Curriculum

  • Diesel Engine Fundamentals
  • Hydro-mechanical Diesel Fuel Injection Systems
  • Computerized Truck and Bus Systems Management
  • Truck Electricity and Electronics
  • Truck Steering, Suspension, and Wheels
  • Truck Brakes
  • Truck Driveline Systems
  • Truck Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) Systems

Certification

Upon completion, students receive a Diesel Mechanic/Heavy Truck Maintenance Certificate. Tuition includes first testing fees for ASE (Automotive Service Excellence) Certification.

Financial Aid

Financial aid may be available for Virginia residents. Please call our office at (276) 794-7660 for more information about eligibility and application.

Admission Requirements

  • Must be 18 years of age or older
  • High school diploma or GED required

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: $21,500 (VA) — includes tuition, books, supplies & ASE exam fees. Financial aid available for Virginia residents.

item.title: NCCER Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tri-County Driving Academy offers NCCER Heavy Equipment Operations Level One training as part of our Heavy Equipment Training program.

Students successfully completing Heavy Equipment Operations Level One through Tri-County Driving Academy earn industry-recognized NCCER credentials verifiable through the NCCER Automated National Registry. Employers across the country recognize and trust NCCER credentials when hiring equipment operators.

About NCCER

The National Center for Construction Education and Research (NCCER) is a not-for-profit education foundation established to develop standardized curricula and assessments for the construction and maintenance industries. NCCER credentials give employers confidence that you have the skills and knowledge needed for safe, productive work.

How to Earn Your NCCER Credential

Enroll in our Certified Heavy Equipment Training program. NCCER certification is included as part of that program's curriculum and tuition.

Call (276) 794-7660 for more information.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Included with Heavy Equipment Training — see Heavy Equipment program for tuition
Included in the 6-week Heavy Equipment Training program

item.title: Virginia Driver Improvement Course
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tri-County Driving Academy offers the Virginia Driver Improvement Course, certified by the Commonwealth of Virginia. This course is acceptable at any Virginia Court that allows driver improvement courses.

Course Details

  • Price: $75
  • Hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
  • Location: 1714 US HWY 19, Lebanon, VA 24266

Schedule

Call our office at (276) 794-7660 for the current schedule and available class dates.

Online Option

Prefer to complete your driver improvement course online? Visit VA Online Driver Improvement Course — available anytime, at your own pace.

item.title: Welcome to the New Tri-County Driving Academy Website
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the new Tri-County Driving Academy website! We have updated our online presence to better serve our students and prospective applicants.

Browse our programs page to learn about our CDL Class A, Heavy Equipment, Diesel Mechanics, and Driver Improvement training programs. For questions, please contact us or call (276) 794-7660.

Financial aid, including Pell Grants, WIOA funding, and Veterans benefits, may be available. Learn more about financial aid options.
item.excerpt:encoded: Welcome to the updated Tri-County Driving Academy website. Browse our programs and contact us today to get your career started.
